statement.en: A Multimodal Text- and Graph-Based Approach for Open-Domain Event Extraction from Documents
statement.summary: Event extraction is essential for event understanding and analysis. It supports tasks such as document summarization and decision-making in emergency scenarios. However, existing event extraction approaches have limitations: (1) closed-domain algorithms are restricted to predefined event types and thus rarely generalize to unseen types and (2) open-domain event extraction algorithms, capable of handling unconstrained event types, have largely overlooked the potential of large language models (LLMs) despite their advanced abilities. Additionally, they do not explicitly model document-level contextual, structural, and semantic reasoning, which are crucial for effective event extraction but remain challenging for LLMs due to lost-in-the-middle phenomenon and attention dilution. To address the

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a preprint (2026-04-25, future-dated or recent submission) describing a novel methodological approach to event extraction. The core contribution—multimodal text- and graph-based open-domain event extraction—is presented as a research proposal addressing known limitations in the field. The abstract is incomplete, preventing full evaluation of claimed results. As an unvalidated preprint, it occupies provisional/under-change status pending peer review, empirical validation, and publication.
